business_definition: A directly measured or collected fact describing an entity or environment.
Knowledge inferred or computed from one or more observations.
A surrounding condition or circumstance influencing business decisions.
A measurable property describing contextual state.
The business entity to which contextual intelligence applies.
Origin from which an observation is obtained.
Supporting information used to justify an intelligence value.
A measurable quantity representing an underlying phenomenon.
A quantitative measurement used in analysis.
An input variable used by analytical or predictive models.
A characteristic describing a subject or context.
A perspective used to organize contextual information.
A meaningful change or event detected from observations.
Potential for adverse outcomes under specified conditions.
Categorical interpretation of a risk score.
Estimated reliability of an intelligence value.
Expected consistency and trustworthiness of information.
Time interval during which intelligence is considered applicable.
Direction and magnitude of change over time.
Observation significantly deviating from expected behaviour.
Method used to derive contextual intelligence.
Method used to calculate normalized scores.
Relative importance assigned to an observation.
Combining multiple observations into a summary value.
Causal or logical influence between contexts.
Relationship where one context influences another.
Transfer of influence through connected contexts.
Index formed by combining multiple metrics.
Degree to which a subject experiences a contextual factor.
Ability to justify an intelligence result using evidence.
State of intelligence at a specific point in time.
Suggested action derived from contextual intelligence.
Context considered while making a recommendation.
Relative importance assigned to an action.
Action performed before adverse events occur.
Action performed after adverse events occur.
Minimum score required to trigger an action.
Traceable reasoning chain behind a decision.
